docs.text: Kotlin Reffrences This is an alternative set of documentation for Kotlin, designed to be simpler and easier to use than traditional resources. The examples included here are generated with the help of a large language model (LLM). While care has been taken to ensure accuracy, there may still be mistakes. If you find any issues or inaccuracies, please feel free to open a pull request with your corrections. Contributions are always welcome! The source can be find on GitHub

docs.text: This was made by Axel Gard I made this becouse I found the kotlin docs to be a little unclear and I was missing exampels at times. So I made this for my own use. I have used GPT-OSS 20B for the exampels. So Im sure there are some mistakes but I will try and fix them in the case that I find them. If you find them, feel free to make a PR or a Issue .
docs.title: About

docs.text: addSuppressed kotlin-stdlib / kotlin / addSuppressed When supported by the platform, adds the specified exception to the list of exceptions that were suppressed in order to deliver this exception. expect fun Throwable . addSuppressed ( exception : Throwable )( source ) fun main () { try { // Primary operation that fails throw IllegalArgumentException ( "Primary exception" ) } catch ( primary : IllegalArgumentException ) { try { // Secondary operation that also fails throw IllegalStateException ( "Suppressed exception" ) } catch ( suppressed : IllegalStateException ) { // Add the suppressed exception to the primary one primary . addSuppressed ( suppressed ) } // Print the stack trace to see the suppressed exception primary . printStackTrace () } } Source
docs.title: addSuppressed

docs.text: also kotlin-stdlib / kotlin / also Calls the specified function block with this value as its argument and returns this value. @IgnorableReturnValueinline fun < T > T . also ( block : ( T ) -> Unit ): T ( source ) fun main () { val numbers = mutableListOf ( 1 , 2 , 3 ). also { println ( "Original list: $ it " ) } numbers . add ( 4 ). also { println ( "After adding 4: $ it " ) } } Source
docs.title: also

docs.text: and kotlin-stdlib / kotlin / and Performs a bitwise AND operation between the two values. infix inline fun BigInteger . and ( other : BigInteger ): BigInteger ( source ) import java.math.BigInteger fun main () { val a = BigInteger ( "0b1101" , 2 ) // 13 val b = BigInteger ( "0b1011" , 2 ) // 11 val result = a and b // bitwise AND println ( result ) // prints 9 } Source
docs.title: and

docs.text: apply kotlin-stdlib / kotlin / apply Calls the specified function block with this value as its receiver and returns this value. @IgnorableReturnValueinline fun < T > T . apply ( block : T .() -> Unit ): T ( source ) data class Person ( var name : String = "" , var age : Int = 0 ) val person = Person (). apply { name = "Alice" age = 28 } Source

docs.text: arrayOf kotlin-stdlib / kotlin / arrayOf Returns an array containing the specified elements. expect inline fun < T > arrayOf ( vararg elements : T ): Array < T > ( source ) fun main () { val ints = arrayOf ( 1 , 2 , 3 , 4 , 5 ) val strings = arrayOf ( "apple" , "banana" , "cherry" ) println ( ints . joinToString ()) // Output: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 println ( strings . joinToString ()) // Output: apple, banana, cherry } Source
docs.title: arrayOf
